headline: Nothing’s first B-series phone is also skipping the US
summary: Nothing unveils its new budget Phone 4B, merging 4A and 4A Pro designs. The new B-series phone will not be released in the US market. This continues Nothing's pattern of skipping the US for its cheaper handsets.

headline: Nothing’s new earbuds can record calls and what you’re listening to
summary: Nothing launched new budget-friendly wireless earbuds, the Ear 3A. These new earbuds feature unique call and audio recording capabilities. Priced at $99, they are available now and match the cost of previous models.

headline: Hoto's PixelDrive screwdriver is down to $60, matching its best price
summary: Hoto's PixelDrive cordless screwdriver is currently on sale for $59.99. This deal at Amazon matches the best historical price for the gadget. It's ideal for assembling furniture or repairing electronics.

headline: America’s greatest idea is still under threat
summary: The United States recently celebrated its 250th anniversary with global festivities. Fireworks and celebrations occurred across the nation and in countries like France and Japan. Despite widespread celebration, the article suggests a core American ideal remains under threat.

headline: 'She'd Be Great as Mystique': Obsession Star Inde Navarette Confirms Meeting With X-Men Director Jake Schreier, as Marvel Fans Discuss Who She's Likely to Play
summary: Obsession actress Inde Navarette confirms a meeting with X-Men reboot director Jake Schreier. Marvel fans are speculating which mutant role she might play in the new film. Many online discussions suggest she could be cast as Mystique.

headline: Microsoft Expected Game Pass Would Have 77 Million Subscribers by Now but It Reportedly Only Has 30 Million, as Xbox Boss Admits the Strategy Has Failed
summary: Microsoft's Game Pass subscriber numbers are significantly below expectations. The service reportedly has 30 million subscribers, far short of the projected 77 million. Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has admitted the Game Pass growth strategy has failed.

headline: Nintendo Confirms Switch 1 Global Availability After Announcing European Withdrawal
summary: Nintendo has confirmed it will continue global sales of the original Switch console. This announcement comes after earlier news that sales would be suspended in Europe. The decision ensures continued availability of the Switch 1 in other markets.

headline: Walmart Has a Decent 512GB Nintendo Switch 2 Memory Card Deal Despite the Price Hikes
summary: Walmart is offering a competitive deal on a 512GB memory card for the Nintendo Switch 2. This offer is notable given recent price increases on storage solutions. Extra memory is crucial for Switch 2 owners to expand game libraries.

headline: Belgium say 'sense of injustice' spurred them on to knock US out of World Cup
summary: Belgium defeated the US in a World Cup last-16 match, with a 'sense of injustice' fueling their victory. Nicolas Raskin stated the team was fired up by FIFA's decision regarding a US player. The controversy centered around allowing US striker Folarin Balogun to play against them.

headline: One million more UK homeowners set to face higher mortgages
summary: An additional one million UK homeowners are projected to face increased mortgage costs. They are expected to pay an average of £45 more per month. These hikes will occur as they move onto new mortgage deals over the next two years.

headline: MI5 knew agent was misogynist 'obsessed' with violence, watchdog finds
summary: A security service watchdog found 'serious failings in MI5's management of Agent X'. The inquiry concluded MI5 was aware the agent was a misogynist. The agent was also known to be 'obsessed' with violence.

headline: Endometriosis diagnosis set to be sped up with two new tests carried out by GPs
summary: Two new tests are expected to significantly accelerate endometriosis diagnosis by GPs. Experts hope these tests will cut the current average nine-year diagnosis wait. The new methods are hailed as a 'game-changer' for patients suffering from the condition.
